concepts.lesson: Specular reflection is a type of reflection where light hits a smooth surface, such as a mirror or still water, at a shallow angle. This results in a clear and sharp image being formed behind the reflective surface. In contrast to diffuse reflection, which scatters light in all directions, specular reflection produces a focused image.
concepts.real_example: A person looking into a mirror sees their own image reflected back.
key_points: Specular reflection occurs when light hits a smooth surface at a shallow angle
It results in a clear and sharp image being formed behind the reflective surface
Diffuse reflection scatters light in all directions

concepts.lesson: The umbra is the darker inner shadow region that occurs when an object blocks a light source. It is the area where the light is completely blocked by the object, resulting in no direct light reaching the surface.
concepts.real_example: A lampshade casting a shadow on the wall.
key_points: Umbra is the darker inner shadow region
It occurs when an object blocks a light source
The umbra is the area where the light is completely blocked

concepts.lesson: A virtual image is formed by light rays that appear to diverge from a point behind the mirror or lens. It is not a real image, but rather an apparent one created by the way light behaves when it hits a reflective surface.
concepts.real_example: When looking into a magnifying glass, you see a virtual image of the object being magnified.
key_points: A virtual image is formed by light rays that appear to diverge from a point behind the mirror or lens
It is not a real image, but rather an apparent one created by light behavior
Virtual images can be inverted or upright
